reviews.review_text: Great game, but if you're in the Middle East, think twice before getting attached to the story. Sony removed the sequel of this game (Horizon Forbidden West) from MENA stores due to PlayStation Network (PSN) account linking policies and regional store locking. Which means players in MENA basically can't access the sequel. So you'll finish this, get invested in Aloy and the whole story, and then hit a wall when you try to continue it in Forbidden West.
Very nice graphics and storyline. The concept is very interesting as in the idea of merging futuristic machines with an ancient (almost primal) world.

Amazing game. Combat's excellent. Story is thrilling with it having my most hated villain in all of gaming. Probably in my top 5 games of all time 9.8/10.  Sad though that Sony won't be porting over future installments.

Pretty bummed out by yet another copy paste of the Ubisoft open world grind formula. This is a single player game with excellent combat, but the worst method of delivering game progression and story imaginable. More than 80% of the story is in these little data codec things that you spend 90% of your time walking around clicking your right stick in just to see them. It's so hard to play any open world game after something like a BOTW or TOTK at this point, because almost everything else follows this crappy, quest marker, MMO-lite formula.

Good, semi-adictive gameplay with good world-building and a cool premise gets bogged down by a meandering story and annoyingly awful characters. As good as the gameplay loop is, it is quite repetitive. I would not NOT recommend it, but I would also not recommend it as a game. If I could rank it I'd say its a solid 6 out of 10. A decent game, not bad enough to be mediocre, and not good enough to be remembered. 

Above all things, this game is beautiful still in 2026.

I can't even describe how much I love this game. I Ican't even put it into words, everything is just perfect, the characters, the story, the gameplay, the machines, everything! And Aloy, is so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ badass to play ass, I feel so cool and I LOVE HER. The machine designs are all amazing, as are the characters and just, everything!!
One of the most beautiful worlds I have ever played. the gameplay is a lot of fun, the story has me hooked. amazing game, 10/10, recommended.
A rich world with a gripping story, and dramatic gameplay.
Great concept and decent execution. The open world feels expansive and the combat is intricate. Upgrade system is interesting and the enemies have a lot of flavor.

This game had massive potential, with excellent stealth, but it was frankly ruined for me by the scripted fail, infinite enemies nonsense of the “Revenge of the Nora” mission, in which you are automatically detected, no matter what, no matter how perfectly stealthy you were, after killing a certain number of enemies.

Frankly, being detected when I wasn't detected, ruins my immersion. I wouldn't recommend this game to anyone.
Absolute amazing game good story looks simply amazing love the characters it's a wonderful game to play everybody should try it

While it provides nothing you wouldn't expect of an open-world RPG mechanics-wise beyond a well-developed combat system, this game really shines in setting and story. All that really cool stuff you see in the trailer? Yes, it's all in there, and every bit as engaging as the trailer makes it look.
Very unique gameplay mechanics, lots of side content to do, definitely recommend!
Fun combat, good story so far. Still looks great for an "older" game.
audio has some serious bugs, but the game is solid even by todays standard.
2nd best game ever after forbidden west. better storyline than any other game ive played. 10/10, this game is the ♥♥♥♥
Bought this years ago and never made it through the tutorial for some reason. Never came back till now. Finally decided to give it another try and it's a great surprise. Love it.

Great story
Control is good (mouse and keyboard)
So far only  had minor issues, fx unable to target and pick up loot in terrain, but nothing serious.
when this came out, it was the biggest sleeper ever. it's now well established and still absolutely amazing mechanics and gameplay and graphics and story and everything. 10/10. masterpiece.
Great story and gameplay - I never felt like I was grinding to level up. Engaging and cinematic.

Alright game, amazing combat (against machines not so much the people). Had to stop playing because of the immense amount of dialogue but came back. If you focus on the main story it becomes more manageable and enjoyable.
Loved the story and loved the characters. Couldn't asked for a better game. Personally my favorite game of all time and in my opinion the best game ever
The graphics are very good but as most games sound balance off. Sound is flat could be much better. If you would like to know how contact me.

Amazing story. good atmosphere, interesting characters, challenges, and weapons/armor system. 
The expansion was a pretty good experience too. 
After finishing both on PlayStation, I bought it again on PC
Great game, runs on ♥♥♥♥♥♥ PC that’s basically on fire 24/7. Still getting 100+ FPS with DLSS Play it

I like this game, it's very good overall though it does have a lot of issues.

A lot of the mechanics are very unreliable, the climbing sometimes doesn't work so I drop to my death for no reason, stealth attacks are iffy when on inclines, I had a mission get stuck because an enemy flew straight up and never came back, quests aren't visible on the map if you aren't near them, your inventory space is just small enough that you are constantly emptying it out even after upgrades, enemy animations are often so fast that hitting specific parts is near impossible, the arrows curve midair to a crazy degree although this never seems to make them land when they wouldn't have already and I'm pretty sure I've seen it miss when it was going to land. The main character does seem to pull information out of thin air quite a lot and it does feel aggravating how useless almost everyone else seems to be without her. Also, not to get all culture war-y but it seems like every problem is caused by stupid, incapable or evil men and strong competent women are always the solution, it happens often enough that it stuck out.

I would still recommend it despite all those issues and various others though.

My opinion of  [I]Horizon[/I] seems to degrade year after year.

I don't know, its like the memory of the game is so much worse than the experience of actually playing it.

Initially, it's great. Excellent and well done premise, I mean, robot dinosaurs and mass extinction events, THE FOLLY OF MAN, all well and good and satisfying. 

Precision shots with the bow? Cool, cool, that's unique and challenging. It's actually a lot of fun, for awhile at least.

I think the root of the problem lies in that it heavily, heavily, HEAVILY, overstays its welcome.

[I]Horizon[/I] doesn't have enough meat on its bones to justify the repeat gameplay loops that get overly spammed. Combat encounters would be better served to be hand made here, but instead we get a swarm of robo-dino encounters that grow annoying enough to bypass the system entirely by chucking bomb arrows at everything. Nor is the writing quality good enough for me to care about repeated desolation on the radio messages, much less the meandry of 'ancient' village life.  I did care initially. By round 7 I couldn't shake the 'yeah yeah no food sucks for you' reaction. 

It's difficult to connect with many of the characters. They're trope-y and single faceted. The most interesting of them gain that status via being egnigmatic, and lose it once their motives are revealed.

Overall, [I]Horizon[/I] has the some of the largest discrepancy in quality of lore versus quality of writing that comes to mind. The background events are amazing, the central hook just straight cool, and I really enjoy the design work.

Post grand mystery reveal though, well. It loses its luster.

By the end [I]Horizon[/I] somehow made robo animals annoying. The game would be better if it was cut in half. I can't say that I hate the game, but if you're gonna play it the game is better with much of it skipped.

i like this game because during my playthrough my middle finger was hurt pretty bad and it didnt change the gameplay at all. anyway my finger was hurt because i flipped off an evil killer and he shot me

Wow, just.. wow. From the trailer and visuals, I expected a graphical experience with an interesting story. Boy, did I underestimate this game.

Horizon, Zero Dawn has the most impactful story of any game I've played. The kind where I had to put the controller down for a while and just adsorb the impact of what I just learnt.

It's one of the games where writers and actors really turns the (awesome, nothing wrong with the game mechanics) "game" into an actual story, and one that just doesn't leave my mind as I finish playing.

One of my _absolute_ all-time favorites. I just can't wait until my daughter grows old enough to play it with me.
love the game. i just wish people would make game with the same amount of dedication and love like these devs did.
A very beatiful game with intense combats, a large map to explore and good gameplay.

i went into this game blind for the overall story and lore. it was beautiful and the graphics great, i only had a few bugs that were visual or funny but nothing game breaking or halting progression. i highly recommend playing this and forbidden west.  its shame the new multiplayer game we all got hyped for is low effort slop but i have hope this series will one day return with the beautiful story, designs, animation and polish that made the game fun to explore.
Cool lore. Fun satisfying gameplay. Wonderful ambience.

This was one of the games that has been sitting in my steam library for 4 years.
Finally took a shot at it, and needless to say, I was blown away. 
The game was absolutely GORGEOUS, and the story was MASTERFUL, taking a very unique concept ("read haired stone-age archer taking on robo-dinosaurs") and making a genuinely amazing story out of it.

lastly, if you do go for it, 3 tips to make the experience better: 
1) Ultra hard is a GRIND for your first playthrough (due to everything costing double, if not more), if you intend to do 2 playthoughs (new game, then New game +), youre better off doing very hard -> ultra hard (use 1 pass to level up, and play ultra hard when you unlock the 4-slot bows)
...or at least in your normal playthrough lower the difficulty BEFORE you talk to a merchant lol, (in a standard playthrough the steam achievements wont penalise you for lowering difficulty, only NG+ is "locked in" once you set it at the very start) so same can be said if youre farming for resources / xp in a std playthrough

2) make sure you get your brightness settings right, during the cave explorations I personally felt a bit dizzy because my lighting was too low.

3) if you are to do ng+, just to be sure everything carries over,  load into your latest savegame and click on "create ng+ loadout" (otherwise you will have the one from right after finishing story, not the work you did after that!).

5/5 stars
My all time favorite series. Great music, best open world mechanics, unique and fun gameplay, story leaves you wondering what is going to happen next, and amazing graphics

Really enjoyed the 20 hours i put into this game, played through all the way on steam deck.
Storyline was a bit mid but the combat and the world was awesome.

8/10
I really liked the main story, the world, and gameplay but I found myself a little bored with the side quests and optional content. Worth a shot.
started playing for the open world and hunting but what kept me playing was the amazing story and characters

Wish I could give a "mid" review since the game on a whole is a 6/10 but I really wouldn't recommend it. but why? well the play space is boring and feels uninspired with notable exceptions of the old earth bunkers which have a distinct art style, the characters are boring at best and annoying at worst and our protagonist Aloy happens to be one of the most insufferable. The first few hours are alright but she never develops never falters never wavers she just is. The reaction of characters around her are (aside from the begining) is a jaring amount of praise. What I do like though is the ideas for the backstory of the world, the robot design and lore for there existance and the whole concept of primitive man facing mechasaruses. The gameplay is also locked in nicely.

If this is your first intro to a open-world story RPG game I can see it being good but if you grew up with story's like Mass Effect or worlds like skyrim you will be bored by this one.

TLDR: World design is uninspired, character writing is stale or insufferable but the concept is there and so is the gameplay.

Aloy is easily one of my favorite protagonists. She's driven and no-nonsense, and Ashly Burch’s performance carries every scene. The world-building is brilliant. Instead of overwhelming you with lore, the game lets you discover it piece by piece through datapoints and ruins. Every little detail rewards your curiosity. The reveal of Elisabet, GAIA, and the true nature of Zero Dawn completely reframed my experience of the game. The ending genuinely moved me.

Sony Interactive Entertainment to no longer release single-player games on PC
Confirmed by Hermen Hulst during company town hall.

I forgot to leave a review about it, and I wanted to share my honest opinion because I still see a lot of people playing it.

I played this around three years ago, I think, and after a while I uninstalled it because of the mixed gameplay between stealth and RPG elements that I didn’t like and I probably would’ve completed it if it had been more linear and not open world, since the open world didn’t really feel necessary.

To me, the game just didn’t make much sense, and the story wasn’t anything special either.

First of all, how are you supposed to destroy giant mechanical animals with arrows? I know there are special techniques involving fire, electricity, and other effects, but I still don’t really get it. It feels like the game design mixes too many elements from different genres without doing any of them particularly well.

Yeah, I got bored and uninstalled it.

Having great environments, lighting, VFX, and beautiful character designs won’t automatically make a game enjoyable or playable.

That said, I don’t think I’ll ever play “Forbidden West”.
I really want to like this game because bow gameplay is my thing ... but man BOW against robots :( why

Game is good, feels like assassins creed odyssey but everything is improved, fun as hell. Character is an insufferable boss ♥♥♥♥♥ with 0 feminine traits, but she doesnt talk too much, so its ok. Recommended!

Boring and unfulfilling. Nothing grips me, and I can't get on board with the the robot animals.
It’s fun for the first 10 hours or so, but after that it starts getting boring.

But, the weponds are cool, the game play (when its day time!) is enjoyable. the diffrent ways to kill the mec's. it could have been a great game imo. but the dev's f it. it was clearly written by a 20 something woke female with no real life exp. the story is garbage. the game play is good. over all i hate it because its always f ing night time! and i can not see half the stuff at night!! i do not recomend this game.

Horizon Zero Dawn Complete Edition completely pulled me into its world faster than I expected. At first I thought the whole “tribal humans fighting robot dinosaurs” idea sounded cool but a little ridiculous — then a few hours later I was fully invested in both the combat and the mystery behind the world.

What hooked me the most was the setting and story. The world feels beautiful, dangerous, and strangely believable despite being filled with giant mechanical creatures trying to turn you into a statistic. I loved slowly uncovering what happened to civilization and why the world ended up the way it did. The deeper the story goes, the more interesting it becomes, and honestly some of the lore discoveries hit harder than I expected.

The combat is probably one of my favorite parts of the game. Fighting machines actually feels tactical instead of just mashing attacks until something falls over. Scanning weak points, setting traps, using elemental arrows, tearing components off enemies — every bigger fight feels like hunting a machine instead of simply fighting it. And when a giant Thunderjaw starts charging at you, the game very quickly reminds you that confidence and survival are not always connected.

I also really liked exploring the world itself. The environments are gorgeous, and every region has its own atmosphere, wildlife, and machine types. Sometimes I’d head toward a quest objective and get completely distracted because I spotted a Tallneck in the distance or accidentally wandered into a machine site way above my level.

Aloy also ended up being a great protagonist for me. She’s smart, determined, and actually feels grounded compared to a lot of overly dramatic RPG characters. Watching her slowly uncover the truth about the world made the story much more engaging.

The Complete Edition adds even more content with The Frozen Wilds, which honestly has some of the best environments and hardest fights in the game. The new machines there felt like the developers looked at normal combat and decided players were getting a little too comfortable.

That said, some side activities can feel repetitive after a while, and certain open-world mechanics definitely show their age compared to newer RPGs. But the core gameplay, atmosphere, and worldbuilding are strong enough that I barely cared most of the time.

For me, Horizon Zero Dawn is one of those games where the more I played, the more invested I became. Between the combat, exploration, worldbuilding, and mystery, it ended up being one of the most memorable open-world games I’ve played in years.

Don't bother buying. PlayStation has stated no more single player games will come to PC. That means games like Spider-Man, God of War, Ghost of Tsushima, Horizon, etc if they get sequels they will not be coming to PC so it is a waste to even buy them. Especially since Ghost of Tsushima already has a sequel released and it will not be coming to PC.

The game is really good. I especially like the story, the world-building, the graphics, the pace, and the scope of the game. Many questions that arise at the beginning and throughout the story are answered as the game progresses. That makes the experience worthwhile.

What I don’t like:
- Sometimes the main character’s attitude comes across as a bit odd.
- RNG modifications, because you constantly have to compare and sort through stats.
- No storage box, which can be a bit tight with 120 slots after the expansion.

I recommend playing the game on Very Hard and NG+ on Ultra Hard, as that’s when the various weapon systems need to be used effectively and your playstyle needs to be adapted. Only then does the sophisticated combat system truly shine.
